title: Ipomoea brevipedicellata
intro: Ipomoea brevipedicellata is a perennial vine native to Africa, belonging to the family Convolvulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ipomoea brevipedicellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to fully validate its therapeutic potential.
taxonomy: Ipomoea brevipedicellata is classified within the family Convolvulaceae, commonly known as the morning glory family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, such as its twining habit and the shape of its flowers and leaves. Ipomoea brevipedicellata belongs to the genus Ipomoea, which comprises around 600 species of flowering plants. The species is further categorized into the section Spigelaii within the genus. This specific classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and diversity within the Ipomoea genus.
uses: Ipomoea brevipedicellata, also known as the small-fruited morning glory, is a plant with potential medicinal uses.
